name: Occupational Personality Questionnaire (OPQ - OPQ32r)
description:
The SHL Occupational Personality Questionnaire, the OPQ32, is one of the
  most widely used and respected measures of workplace behavioural style in the
  world. It sets a high standard of measurement excellence, providing HR
  professionals and business managers with relevant and accurate information to
  make fast and well-informed people decisions. The OPQ32 provides a clear
  framework for understanding the impact of personality on job performance. it
  is internationally recognised for its accuracy of assessment. Over 90
  independent validation studies have been conducted on the OPQ over a period
  of 25 years, across 20 countries and 40 industries, providing concrete
  evidence of its power to predict performance in the workplace.

name: .NET Framework 3.5
description:
The .NET Framework 3.5 test measures knowledge of the .NET environment.
  Designed for experienced users, this test covers the following topics: .NET
  Architectural Overview, .NET Framework Class Library, .NET Metadata and
  Windows Workflow Foundation, .NET Programming, Assemblies, CLS, CTS and LINQ,
  Common Language Runtime (CLR), Data Service Components, and Presentation
  Layer.

name: .NET Framework 3.5 Fundamentals
description:
The .NET Framework 3.5 Fundamentals test measures knowledge of the .NET
  3.5 environment. Designed for experienced users, this test covers the
  following topics: .NET Assemblies, Advanced Topics, Configuration and
  Maintenance, Data Access, Framework Programming, Graphics and User Interface
  Development, Processing Data and Text, and Testing and Debugging.

name: .NET Framework Fundamentals
description:
The .NET Framework Fundamentals test measures knowledge of the .NET
  environment. Designed for experienced users, this test covers the following
  topics: .NET Architectural Overview, .NET Framework Class Library, .NET
  Programming, Assemblies, Common Language Runtime (CLR), and Common Type
  System (CTS).

name: Accounts Payable Fundamentals
description:
This adaptive test is part of the accounting fundamentals line of tests.
  This test measures knowledge of processing payables and vendor invoices and
  the posting of journal entries. Designed for accounting clerks and others who
  handle the processing of accounts payable, this test covers the following
  topics: Assets, Cash Management/Reconciliation, Controls, Expenses,
  Purchases, and Recording. The test taker will need a calculator when taking
  this exam.

name: Accounts Receivable/Billing Fundamentals
description:
The Accounts Receivable/Billing Fundamentals test measures basic
  knowledge of processing receivables and invoices. Designed for accounting
  clerks and others who handle the processing of accounts payable, this test
  covers the following topics: A/R Principles, Applying Cash, Bad
  Debt/Collections, Cash Flow, Credit, General Ledger, Invoicing, and
  Reconciliation of Customer Accounts. The test taker will need a calculator
  when taking this exam.

name: ADO.NET 2.0
description:
The ADO.NET 2.0 test measures knowledge of programming using ADO within
  the .NET framework. Designed for .NET developers, this test covers the
  following topics: Components, DataBindings, Datasources, Implementation,
  Object Model, Performance, Transactions, and XML.

name: AJAX
description:
The AJAX test measures knowledge of developing webpages using AJAX.
  Designed for experienced Web developers, programmers, and other IT
  professionals, this test covers the following topics: Communication, DOM
  Manipulation, Event Management, Graceful Degradation, Performance, Quality
  Assurance, Security, Usage, and Visual Elements.

name: Apache 2.0 Administration
description:
The Apache 2.0 Administration test measures knowledge of the Apache HTTP
  Server 2.0. Designed for experienced users, this test covers the following
  topics: Customization, Dynamic Content, Manipulating URL, Modules,
  Performance Optimization, Securing Web Server, Server Configuration, Setup,
  Tracking Server Activities, and Virtual Hosts.

name: ASP.NET 2.0
description:
The ASP.NET 2.0 test measures knowledge of programming in the .NET
  framework. Designed for experienced developers, this test covers the
  following topics: .NET Framework, Architecture, Data Integration, Events,
  Troubleshooting, Web Applications, Web Controls, Web Forms, and Web Services.

name: ASP.NET 3.5
description:
The ASP.NET 3.5 test measures knowledge of programming in the .NET
  framework. Designed for experienced developers, this test covers the
  following topics: Architecture and Support, ASP.NET Controls, ASP.NET
  Enhancements, ASP.NET Essentials, Data Management, Design and Visual Media,
  Extending ASP.NET, and Web Services.

name: ASP.NET 3.5 Fundamentals
description:
The ASP.NET Fundamentals test measures knowledge of programming in the
  .NET framework. Designed for entry-level programmers, this test covers the
  following topics: .NET Framework, Architecture, Data Integration, Events,
  Troubleshooting, Web Applications, Web Controls, and Web Forms.

name: ASP.NET Fundamentals
description:
The ASP.NET test measures knowledge of developing websites using Active
  Server Pages in the .NET framework. Designed for experienced developers, this
  test covers the following topics: .NET Framework, Architecture, Data
  Integration, Events, Troubleshooting, Web Applications, Web Controls, Web
  Forms, and Web Services.

name: Assembly Language
description:
The Assembly Language test measures knowledge of the concepts used in all
  areas of assembly language. Designed for experienced programmers, this test
  covers the following topics: Addressing Modes, Arithmetic Instructions, Bit
  Manipulations, Data and Instructions, Input/Output, Interrupts, Numbering and
  Representations, Programmers View, and Transfer of Control.

name: Bash Shell Scripting
description:
The Bash Shell Scripting test measures knowledge of Bash Shell Scripting
  Version 2. Designed for experienced scripting professionals, this test covers
  the following topics: Built-in Commands, Parameters, Precedence and
  Evaluation, Program Control, Programming Constructs, Redirection, Shell
  Environment, and Using the Command Line.

name: BEA WebLogic Application Server 8.1 Admin
description:
The BEA WebLogic Application Server 8.1 Administration test measures
  knowledge of administering an application server using the BEA WebLogic
  Server. Designed for experienced network administrators, this test covers the
  following topics: Application and Deployment, Pre-Planning/Pre-Installation,
  Server Configuration, Server Management, Server Monitoring, Server
  Troubleshooting, and Software Installation.

name: Bookkeeping Fundamentals
description:
This test is adaptive and measures knowledge of bookkeeping tasks and
  functions. Designed for accounting clerks and others who handle bookkeeping
  tasks, this test covers the following topics: Accounts Payable, Accounts
  Receivable, Adjusting Entries, Business Math Conversions, Cash, Financial
  Statements, Fixed Assets, Inventories, Liabilities, Payroll, Recording
  Transactions, and Taxes. A calculator is needed when taking this test.

name: Business Communication (adaptive)
description:
This is an adaptive test that measures knowledge of communicating in the
  workplace. It measures the skills necessary to communicate effectively with
  coworkers at all levels and with external business contacts. Designed for the
  average business worker, this test includes the following topics: Electronic
  Communication, Employment Communication, Listening, Meetings, Nonverbal
  Communication, Verbal Communication, and Written Communication.

name: Business Communications Writing Sample
description:
This instrument aids in the assessment of a candidate's writing
  proficiency and requires the candidate to compose original answers to
  job-related scenarios.

name:
Business Communications Writing Sample
  Insurance
description:
This instrument assesses the candidate's writing proficiency and requires
  the candidate to compose original answers to insurance industry-related
  scenarios.

name: Business Letter Compose
description:
This test is designed to measure typing ability, including spacing and
  formatting. Additionally, this test measures text errors, including spelling
  mistakes. This is a timed test and it begins when the candidate selects OK on
  the computer screen. Candidates are provided a printed text original that
  contains the unformatted text they will need to complete the test: address
  information, greeting, body text, and closing information. Candidates are
  asked to type the correctly formatted version of the text into a word
  processing program. Note that the same text is used in both the Business
  Letter Compose and Business Letter Edit tests. Therefore, we advise clients
  not to use both tests in a given test session as experience with one of the
  tests may influence scores on the other. 
  This test is available in US English, UK English and Australian
  English.

name: Business Letter Edit
description:
This test is designed to measure a candidates ability to accurately make
  corrections from a business letter containing punctuation, spelling, and
  formatting errors. In this assessment, the text of the letter appears in the
  word processing program. Candidates are instructed to correctly format and
  edit the letter provided in the program, rather than having to type in the
  letter from a text original. Note that the same text is used in both the
  Business Letter Compose and Business Letter Edit tests. Therefore, we advise
  clients not to use both tests in a given test session as experience with one
  of the tests may influence scores on the other.  This test is available in US English, UK
  English and Australian English.

name: Business Math
description:
This test measures knowledge of mathematics commonly used in
  business.  Designed for all
  professionals, this test includes the following topics:  Calculating Markup and Markdown Percentage;
  Interest, Invoicing, and Net/Gross; Markdown Price; Markup Price; Original
  Price Before Markdown or Markup; Price from Total without and with Tax; and
  Total from Algorithm, Chart, or Formula.

name: Business Vocabulary
description:
This assessment identifies the ability to correctly identify words common
  in business communications. In this test, the test taker reads a sentence
  containing a highlighted word. The test taker is required to select the
  synonym of the highlighted word from a list of four choices.    This test is available in US English and
  UK English.

name: Business Writing
description:
This test, designed for administrative professionals, measures knowledge
  of important types of business writing being used today.  It covers the following topics: Business
  Letters, Other Formats, Principles of Organization, Rules of Grammar,
  Spelling, Style and Tone, and Vocabulary.

name: C# 3.0 Fundamentals
description:
The C# 3.0 Fundamentals test measures knowledge of programming in the
  .NET framework using C# 3.0. Designed for entry-level programmers, this test
  covers the following topics: Arrays, Generics, and Collections, Class
  Members, Delegates and Events, Exception Handling, Expressions, Grammar and
  Logic, Operators and Overloading, and Reference and Value Types.

name: C++ Fundamentals
description:
The C++ Fundamentals test measures knowledge of programming in C++.
  Designed for entry-level programmers, this test covers the following topics:
  Access Checking, Basic Structure, Classes, Declarations, Declarators,
  Derivation, Exceptions, Expressions, Overloading, Special Member Functions,
  Standard C++ Library, Statements, and Templates.

name: C++
description:
The C++ test measures knowledge of the object-oriented programming
  language C++. Designed for experienced programmers, this test covers the
  following topics: Basic Structure and Syntax, C++ Class, C++ Data Types,
  Class Derivation and Inheritance, Class Member Functions, Exception Handling,
  Fundamentals of OOD, and OOP.

name: Call Centre General Terms
description:
In this assessment, the test taker listens to a term and types it into
  the field on the screen. It measures ability to listen and to accurately
  spell standard English terms. This test contains one question with 25
  terms.  This test is available in US
  English, UK English and Spanish.

name: Call Centre Place Names
description:
This assessment measures ability to listen and to accurately spell
  standard U.S. place names. The test taker listens to the name of a place and
  types it into the field on the screen. It contains one question with 25
  terms.  This test is available in US
  English and UK English.

name: Check Point FireWall-1 NG Administration
description:
The Check Point FireWall-1 NG Administration test measures knowledge of
  designing and maintaining firewalls using Check Point's FireWall-1
  application. Designed for experienced Web administrators, this test covers
  the following topics: Audits, Authentication, Core Concepts, FW-1
  Installation/Architecture, FW-1 Management, High Availability, Network
  Address Translation, Performance Tuning, and Security Policy/Rule Base.

name: CICS
description:
The CICS test measures knowledge of programming using the transaction
  server, Customer Information Control System (CICS). Designed for experienced
  programmers, this test covers the following topics: Basic Mapping Support,
  Data Management, Environment, Management Functions, Program Control, System
  Management Utilities, Testing and Debugging, and Web Services.

name: Cisco Network Design
description:
The Cisco Network Design test measures knowledge of installing and
  configuring an Internet Protocol network using Cisco routers. Designed for
  experienced telecommunications engineers, this test covers the following
  topics: Cisco Network Design Model, Distance Vector Routing Protocols,
  Enterprise Routing Protocols, Multicast Protocols, and OSI Model.

name: Cisco Network Support
description:
The Cisco Network Support test measures knowledge of maintaining an
  Internet Protocol network using Cisco routers. Designed for experienced
  telecommunications engineers, this test covers the following topics: Dial
  Solutions, Hardware Architecture, IOS/Command Line, IP Addressing, LAN
  Switching, Network Protocols, OSI Model, Routing Protocols, Traffic
  Management, and WAN Connectivity.

name: Cisco Networking Concepts
description:
The Cisco Networking Concepts test measures knowledge of implementing,
  maintaining, configuring, securing, and troubleshooting a network using
  Cisco. Designed for experienced users who have responsibilities in systems
  and/or networks that use Cisco, this test covers the following topics: IP,
  LAN Switching, Management and Monitoring, Network Design, Network Security,
  Network Troubleshooting, Networking Models, and WAN.

name: Cisco Router Fundamentals
description:
The Cisco Router Fundamentals test measures knowledge of Routing
  Protocols for Cisco systems. Designed for entry-level users, this test covers
  the following topics: Network Design and Communication, Network Security and
  Management, Routing Protocols, and WAN Technologies.

name: Cisco Security
description:
The Cisco Security test measures knowledge of computer and network
  security in a Cisco system. Designed for experienced IT professionals who
  have network security responsibilities and run a Cisco environment, this test
  covers the following topics: Appliances, Firewalls, IPS/IDS Management,
  Secure Network Management, Secure Routing, Security Information, Security
  Protocols, Security Technologies, and VPN Management.

name: Citrix Administration
description:
The Citrix Administration test measures knowledge of administering a
  system using Citrix WinFrame and MetaFrame. Designed for experienced system
  administrators, this test covers the following topics: Administration,
  Applications, Client Computers, Configuration, General Knowledge,
  Installation, and Troubleshooting.

name: Client/Server Concepts
description:
The Client/Server Concepts test measures knowledge of client/server
  networks. Designed for entry-level users, this test covers the following
  topics: Business Issues, Database, General Knowledge, Network, Processing,
  and Technical Issues.

name: Cognos Development
description:
The Cognos Development test measures knowledge of implementation and
  maintenance of Cognos business intelligence development suite version 8.
  Designed for experienced developers, this test covers the following topics:
  Framework Manager, Powerplay, Powerplay Web, ReportNet, and Transformer.

name: Computer Electronics
description:
The Computer Electronics test measures knowledge of the core areas of
  electronics necessary to comprehend and maintain computers. Designed for
  computer technicians, this test covers the following topics: Diagnostics,
  Electrical/Electronic Engineering Principles, Electronics Application,
  Electronics Theory, and Safety.

name: Computer Forensics
description:
The Computer Forensics (U.S.) test measures knowledge of Computer
  Forensics. Designed for experienced users, this test covers the following
  topics: Analyzing Evidence, Collecting Evidence, Data Types, Examination
  Procedures, Forensic Tools, Initial Analysis, Investigative Preparation, and
  Report Findings.

name: Computer Fundamentals Mac OS X (adaptive)
description:
The Computer Fundamentals - (Mac OS X) test measures knowledge of using a
  Macintosh computer that runs operating system X. Designed for all computer
  users, this test covers the following topics: Apple Menu, Applications,
  Classic, Dock and Desktop, Files and Folders, Finder/Windowing, Networking,
  Printing, System Preferences, Troubleshooting, and Users.

name: Computer Technical Support
description:
The Computer Technical Support test measures knowledge of setting up and
  maintaining a PC. Designed for technicians with hands-on experience, this
  test covers the following topics: External Physical, File Systems, Industry
  Standards, Internal Physical, Internet, Operating Systems, Support, and
  Working with Hardware.

name: Computer Telephony Integration (CTI)
description:
The Computer Telephony Integration (CTI) test measures knowledge of
  designing and installing computer-operated telephone networks in call centers
  and other locations. Designed for experienced CTI professionals, this test
  covers the following topics: Administration, Applications, Architecture,
  Computing, Database, Design Concepts, Disaster Recovery, Telephone User
  Interface, Telephony, and Troubleshooting.

name: Contact Center Virtual Scenario
description:
This assessment is a simulation and is designed mainly for entry-level
  positions in a contact center environment. Sample tasks for these jobs
  include: Interact with customers on the phone to provide information, take
  orders, and solve product or service issues; respond positively to difficult
  or irate customers; navigate within multiple open applications to find and
  view customer account details; and type information quickly and accurately.
  Potential job titles that use this solution are: Call Center Representative,
  Contact Center Representative, Customer Service Agent, and Customer Service
  Representative.  This assessment is
  available in US English, UK English, 
  Australian English and Bilingual Spanish.

name:
Contact Centre Agent Advanced (Short
  Form)
description:
The Contact Center Agent - Advanced solution is an assessment used for
  job candidates applying to entry-level 
  positions in a contact center environment that require more experience
  and where the main focus of the job is customer service. Sample tasks for
  these jobs include, but are not limited to: interacting with customers on the
  phone to provide information; taking orders, and solving product or service
  issues; responding positively to difficult or irate customers; navigating
  within multiple applications to find and view customer account details; and
  typing information quickly and accurately. Potential job titles that use this
  solution are: Call Center Representative, Contact Center Representative,
  Customer Service Agent, and Customer Service Representative. There are
  multiple configurations and versions of this solution available.

name: Contact Centre Retention Predictor
description:
This Contact Center Retention Predictor is designed to identify those who
  are likely to stay in contact center positions for at least 90 days. This
  assessment consists of a variety of item types and is not intended to predict
  on-the-job performance. Short and long forms of the Contact Center Retention
  Predictor are available. The assessment consists of two independently
  developed and validated scales: Contact Center Tenure Scale and Contact
  Center Loyalty Scale.

name:
Contact Centre Team Lead/Coach (Short
  Form)
description:
The Contact Center - Team Lead/Coach solution is for job candidates
  applying to entry-level leadership 
  positions who tend to supervise hourly employees. Sample tasks for
  these jobs include, but are not limited 
  to: planning and preparing work schedules; assigning employees to
  specific duties; coaching employees on 
  attendance, conduct, schedule adherence, and work tasks; developing employees'
  skills; training subordinates  or less
  tenured peers; prioritizing multiple tasks and priorities; and making
  day-to-day decisions with minimal 
  guidance from others. Potential job titles that use this solution are:
  Team Leader, Coach, First Line Supervisor, 
  Processing Supervisor, Call Center Supervisor, and Customer Service
  Supervisor. There are multiple configurations and versions of this solution
  available.

name: CORBA 2.3 C++
description:
The CORBA 2.3 C++ test measures knowledge of programming using CORBA 2.3
  C++. Designed for experienced programmers, this test covers the following
  topics: CORBA Basics, CORBA Services, Design Issues, IDL, Interface
  Repository, Interoperability, Method Invocation, OMA, and ORB Interface.

name: Customer Care Simulation
description:
The Customer Care Simulation test measures speed and accuracy at
  listening and entering information in a simulated call center environment.
  Within a test, two types of calls are simulated. The first type simulates a
  customer requesting information regarding products, and the second type
  simulates a customer ordering products. Test takers are provided one practice
  call of each type, and four or eight calls of each type are scored as part of
  the Expanded version. Different actors are used in each call, and the accent
  of the caller is varied across calls.

name: Data Entry 10 Key Split Screen
description:
Data Entry Ten Key Split Screen assessment measures ability to enter
  numbers using a numeric keypad. The test measures accuracy and speed.

name: Data Entry Alphanumeric Form 1
description:
This test assesses a candidate's ability to enter alphabetical and
  numerical information into fields within a simulated screen on the computer.
  Candidates are provided a printed text original that contains tables of
  information that they must enter exactly into a similar table in a word
  processing document using a keyboard. The information includes
  business-related text and numbers such as office number, company name,
  address, mail code, and billing code. The test assesses for speed and
  accuracy.  This test is available in US
  English, UK English and Australian English.

name: Data Entry Alphanumeric Form 2
description:
This test assesses a candidate's ability to enter alphabetical and
  numerical information into fields within a simulated screen on the computer.
  Candidates are provided a printed text original that contains tables of
  information that they must enter exactly into a similar table in a word
  processing document using a keyboard. The information includes
  business-related text and numbers such as office number, company name,
  address, mail code, and billing code. The test assesses for speed and
  accuracy.  This test is available in US
  English, UK English and Australian English.

name: Data Entry Alphanumeric Split Screen
description:
The Data Entry Alphanumeric Split Screen - US assessment measures speed
  and accuracy at typing text and numbers into forms. The information includes
  business-related text and numbers such as invoice number, address, product
  number and amount. The test assesses for speed and accuracy.

name: Data Entry Numeric Form 1
description:
This test measures the speed and accuracy at typing numbers into forms.
  Test takers are provided a printed text original that contains tables of
  information that they must enter exactly into a similar table in a word
  processing document using a keyboard. The information candidates must enter
  includes business-related records including numeric fields such as item
  number, model number, and quantity. Candidates may use either the keyboards
  numeric keypad or the number keys at the top of the keyboard. The test has a
  total of 26 records, and it is designed such that most candidates will not
  complete the test.  This test is
  available in US English, UK English, and Australian English.

name: Data Entry Numeric Form 2
description:
This test measures the speed and accuracy at typing numbers into forms.
  Test takers are provided a printed text original that contains tables of
  information that they must enter exactly into a similar table in a word
  processing document using a keyboard. The information candidates must enter
  includes business-related records including numeric fields such as item
  number, model number, and quantity. Candidates may use either the keyboards
  numeric keypad or the number keys at the top of the keyboard. The test has a
  total of 26 records, and it is designed such that most candidates will not
  complete the test.  This test is
  available in US English, UK English, and Australian English.

name: DB2 Administration (z/OS)
description:
The DB2 Administration (z/OS) test measures knowledge of administering
  DB2 databases. Designed for experienced administrators, this test covers the
  following topics: Application Program Features, Data Sharing, Database
  Design, Environment, Locking and Concurrency, Monitoring and Tuning,
  Operation and Recovery, SQL, Stored Procedures, Triggers and User-Defined
  Functions, and Utilities and Commands.

name: DB2 Programming
description:
The DB2 Programming test measures knowledge of programming DB2 databases.
  Designed for experienced programmers, this test covers the following topics:
  Adding/Changing Data, Data Access, Data Definition and Data Types, Error
  Handling, Locking, Concurrency, and Unit of Work, Multiple Table Access,
  Performance Considerations, SQL Functions, and Stored Procedures and
  User-Defined Functions.

name: Dependability and Safety Instrument (DSI)
description:
The DSI is a short pre-screening tool for many key entry-level roles. It
  is designed to identify potential employees who will have good dependability
  and reliability, and who are less likely to engage in counter-productive work
  behaviors.

name: Disaster Recovery and Planning
description:
The Disaster Recovery and Planning test measures knowledge of recovery
  techniques in the event of a disaster. Designed for administrators, this test
  covers the following topics: Asset Management, Backups and Offsite Storage,
  Communications, Disaster Prevention (Avoidance), Essential Concepts and
  Terminology, Exposure and Risk Analysis, Planning Fundamentals, Preparation
  Essentials, Recovery/Interim Restoration, and Relocation to Primary
  Site/Final Restoration.

name: Diversity Awareness
description:
The Diversity Awareness (U.S.) test measures knowledge of the issues
  around working in a diverse workplace. Designed for all employees, this test
  covers the following topics: Diversity in Organizations, Diversity - Concepts
  and Driving Forces, Managing for Diversity - Policies and Programs,
  Organizational Roles and Culture, and The Character of Diversity.

name: Dynamic HTML
description:
The Dynamic HTML test measures knowledge of coding webpages using Dynamic
  HTML. Designed for experienced coders, this test covers the following topics:
  Containers, CSS, DOM, Events, Forms, Frames, HEAD Elements, Sights and
  Sounds, and Tables.

name: Editing + Proofing (Chicago Style)
description:
This test measures knowledge centered on the Chicago Manual of Style.
  Designed for writers and editors, this test covers the following topics:
  Common Structural Areas, Common Errors, Numbers, Proofreading, Punctuation,
  and Substantive Editing.

name: Electronic Data Interchange (EDI)
description:
The Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) test measures knowledge of the
  electronic communication of business transactions. Designed for EDI
  professionals, this test covers the following topics: Documentation, General,
  Programming, Security/Auditing, and Standards.

name: Embedded Systems Concepts
description:
The Embedded Systems Concepts test measures knowledge of the core
  concepts involved in working with embedded systems. Designed for all
  professionals involved with embedded systems, this test covers the following
  topics: Debugging, Hardware Design, Interrupts, Software Architecture,
  Software Design, System Design, System Resources, and Validation.

name: English Language Comprehension
description:
This assessment measures ability to understand the English language as it
  is used in business settings. It covers interpersonal communication, business
  jargon, instructions and directions, and business communication.

name: English Vocabulary
description:
This test measures knowledge of English vocabulary. Designed for all
  English speakers, this test covers the following topics: Analogies,
  Challenging words, Foreign words used in the English language, and
  Professional terms.

name: ERP Concepts
description:
The ERP Concepts (U.S.) test measures knowledge of the Enterprise
  Resource Planning (ERP) business management system and methodology. Designed
  for experienced planning professionals, this test covers the following
  topics: Change Management, Enterprise Integration, Enterprise Management, ERP
  Selection, and Implementation.

name: Fiber Optics
description:
The Fiber Optics test measures knowledge of the concepts and practices of
  installing fiber optics. Designed for experienced telecommunications
  engineers and network planners, this test covers the following topics: Fiber
  Cabling for Gigabit Ethernet, Fiber Connectors, Fiber Optic Applications,
  Fiber Optic Basics, Fiber Optic Construction, Fiber Optic Hardware, Fiber
  Optic Specifications, Fiber Optic Transmitters, Fiber Splices, and Testing
  Fiber Optics.

name: Financial Accounting
description:
This test measures knowledge of American financial accounting rules and
  procedures.  Designed for the
  experienced accounting professional, this test covers the following topics:
  A/R and A/P, Budgeting, Cost Accounting, GAAP, Journal Entries, and Tax and
  Payroll.

name: Financial Analysis
description:
This test measures knowledge of financial analysis in the United States
  including the metrics used and the regulatory organizations involved.  Designed for the experienced financial
  professional, this test covers the following topics: Analyzing Return,
  Analyzing Risk, Budgetary Control, Budgets, Cost of Capital, Financial
  Analysis, and Valuation Methods. 
  Test-takers will need a financial calculator for this exam.

name: Financial Management
description:
This test measures knowledge of how companies manage their internal
  finances.  This test covers the
  following topics: Capital Budgeting, Capital Structure, Financial Statements,
  Financing, Internal Reporting, Planning and Control, Market Concepts, Risk
  Concepts, and Valuation Concepts.

name:
Financial Services Representative (Short
  Form)
description:
The Financial Services Representative solution is for senior-level
  professional positions in the insurance industry that sell life insurance to
  businesses and individuals on behalf of insurance companies. Sample tasks for
  this position include, but are not limited to: calling on policyholders to
  deliver and explain policies or suggest additions or changes; calculating
  premiums; customizing insurance programs to suite individual customers;
  seeking out new clients and developing clientele by networking; ensuring
  policy requirements are fulfilled; and conferring with clients to obtain and
  provide information when calims are made on a policy. Potential job titles
  that use this solution are: Life Insurance Sales Professional, Insurance
  Sales Agent. Multiple configurations of this solution are available.

name: Firewall Administration Concepts
description:
The Firewall Administration Concepts test measures knowledge of designing
  and maintaining firewalls. Designed for experienced network administrators,
  this test covers the following topics: Audits, Core Technologies, Internals,
  Maintenance, Network Address Translation (NAT), Optimization, Piercing,
  Policies, Security Vulnerabilities, and Types.

name: Following Instructions (R2)
description:
This test measures ability to follow detailed instructions and then
  select the correct course of action. Test takers are presented with a set of
  rules and need to choose the appropriate response for various situations
  based on the rules given.  This test is
  available in US English and UK English.

name: General Clerical Grammar (R1)
description:
This assessment measures ability to correctly use the various parts of
  speech in written communication and covers subject-verb agreement, sentence
  structure, and punctuation.

name: General Clerical Spelling
description:
This is a multiple choice assessment that measures ability to identify
  correctly spelled words in written communication. It is a timed assessment
  that covers the following topics: plurals, prefixes, suffixes, abbreviations,
  and common errors. There is also a short answer version of the test.

name: General Clerical Spelling short answer
description:
This is the same as the multiple choice version of the test but is
  administered in short answer form. It measures ability to correctly spell
  words in written communication and covers plurals, prefixes, suffixes,
  abbreviations, and common errors.

name: HIPAA (Security)
description:
The HIPAA (Security) test measures knowledge of compliance with the
  standards required by the Security and Electronic Signature Standards as they
  apply to HIPAA. Designed for healthcare professionals and concentrating on
  nontechnical as well as technical aspects of the HIPAA Standards for Security
  and Electronic Signatures, this test covers the following topics: Computer
  Mechanisms, Computer Models, General Knowledge, Implementation, Medical
  Records, Organization, Security Basics, and Setup.

name: Human Resources Concepts
description:
The Human Resources Concepts (U.S.) test measures knowledge of the core
  areas of Human Resources. Designed for human resource professionals and
  experienced managers, this test covers the following topics: Benefits,
  Compensation, Job Analysis, Labor Relations, Managing Employees, Occupational
  Safety and Health, Recruiting, Strategic HR, and Training and Development.

name: IMS 6.0
description:
The IMS 6.0 test measures knowledge of the IBM hierarchical database
  management system. Designed for experienced database managers and system
  engineers, this test covers the following topics: Command Codes, Database
  Description, Database Interface, Deleting/Replacing Segments, DL/I
  Processing, Loading/Inserting Segments, Processing Interface, Random
  Retrieval, Retrieval Calls, and Sequential Retrieval.

name: Informatica PowerMart/PowerCentre 6.2.1
description:
The Informatica PowerMart/PowerCenter 6.2.1 test measures knowledge of
  developing using Informatica 6.2.1. Designed for experienced developers, this
  test covers the following topics: Advanced Features and Functions, Designer,
  Environment Configuration, Mapping Designer, Performance, Repository Manager
  Tasks, Features, and Functions, Setup and Installation, Source Analysis,
  Warehouse Designer, and Workflow Administration Tasks.

name: Information Technology Terminology
description:
The Information Technology Terminology test measures knowledge of the
  terms associated with the IT industry. Designed for all IT professionals,
  this test covers the following topics: Data Communications, Data Security,
  Data Storage Standards and Practices, Internet and WWW Standards and
  Practices, IT Management, Network Topology, Operating Systems Standards and
  Practices, PC Hardware, and Portable Storage.

name: IP Routing + Switching
description:
The IP Routing & Switching test measures knowledge of the protocols
  and concepts used by routers and switches that employ Internet protocol.
  Designed for experienced WAN engineers, this test covers the following
  topics: Advanced Routing Topics, General Knowledge, IP Addressing, IP
  Multicast, IP Routing Protocols, and Layer 2 Switching.

name: ITIL Concepts
description:
The ITIL Concepts test measures knowledge of Information Technology
  Infrastructure Library (ITIL). Designed for individuals who have taken a
  foundation level course or worked in an environment that employs ITIL
  practices, this test covers the following topics: Availability Management,
  Capacity Management, Change Management, Configuration Management, Financial
  Management for IT Services, IT Service Continuity Management, Incident
  Management and Service Desk, Problem Management, Release Management, and Service
  Level Management.

name: Java Server Pages (JSP 2.1)
description:
The Java Server Pages (JSP 2.1) test measures knowledge of programming
  JSP 2.1. Designed for experienced Web programmers and designers, this test
  covers the following topics: Custom Tag Development, JSP Model, Page
  Development, Servlet Development, Servlet Model, Tag Libraries, Tag Usage,
  Web Application Security, and Web Applications.

name: Java XML Technologies
description:
The Java XML Technologies test measures knowledge of the Java API for XML
  Processing. Designed for experienced Java developers, Web architects, and
  software engineers, this test covers the following topics: DOM API -
  Navigation, DOM API - Reading, DOM API - Writing, JAXP API - Processing, JAXP
  API - Transform, Other APIs, SAX 2 API - Error Handling, and SAX 2 API -
  Parsing. This test covers J2SE 1.2 and 1.3.

name: JavaScript 1.5
description:
The JavaScript 1.5 test measures knowledge of programming using
  JavaScript 1.5. Designed for experienced developers, this test covers the
  following topics: Basic JavaScript, Core Objects, Events and Event Handling,
  Flow Control, Functions, General JavaScript, Objects, Regular Expressions,
  and Working with HTML.

name: JavaScript 1.5 Fundamentals
description:
The JavaScript 1.5 Fundamentals test measures knowledge of programming
  using JavaScript 1.5. Designed for entry-level developers, this test covers
  the following topics: Basic JavaScript, Core Objects, Events and Event
  Handling, Flow Control, Functions, General JavaScript, Regular Expressions,
  and Working with HTML.

name: Lotus Domino R5 Programming
description:
The Lotus Domino R5 Programming test measures knowledge of programming in
  a Lotus Domino R5 environment. Designed for experienced programmers, this
  test covers the following topics: Basic Web Design, Core Concepts, Design
  Elements, Formulas, Security/Database, Security/Programmed, and Workflow
  Design.

name: Marketing Concepts
description:
This adaptive test measures the candidate's knowledge of the core areas
  of marketing. Designed for all professionals, this test covers the following
  topics: Advertising & Sales Promotion, Buying Behavior, Demographic
  Decision-Making, Distribution & Channel Selection, Information Analysis,
  Internal & External Company Evaluation, Market Analysis, Marketing
  Control Systems, Marketing Plans, New Product/Service Development, Pricing,
  and Selling Issues.

name: Marketing Strategy
description:
This test is adaptive and measures the candidate's knowledge of the
  strategic issues involved in developing a marketing plan. It is designed for
  experienced marketing professionals and covers the following topics:
  Planning, Assessing Opportunities, Segmentation, Satisfying Customers, Cost
  of Entry, and Integration.

name: Matching
description: The Matching test measures knowledge of accurately comparing information.

name: Mathematical Problem Solving
description:
This assessment measures ability to use basic mathematic computations to
  solve real-world problems. Calculators are not allowed, but candidates can
  use paper and pencil to answer questions.

name: Math-Intermediate Skills
description:
This test measures ability to quickly perform intermediate level
  mathematical computations including addition, subtraction, multiplication,
  and division, as well as calculate percentages and convert fractions and
  decimals. Test takers must answer each question by performing the appropriate
  computation and selecting the correct answer. Test takers are not allowed to
  use calculators, but may use pencil and paper to solve problems.

name: Microsoft ISA Server 2000 Administration
description:
The Microsoft ISA Server 2000 Administration test measures knowledge of
  administering a server running Microsoft ISA Server 2000. Designed for
  experienced network administrators, this test covers the following topics:
  Caching, Client Installation, Installation, Monitoring and Reporting, Rules
  and Policies, Server Publishing, Structure and Security Concepts, and
  Troubleshooting.

name: Microsoft Security
description:
The Microsoft Security test measures knowledge of various aspects of
  security including networks, operating systems, security control concepts,
  servers, and client computers. Designed for experienced administrators, this
  test covers the following topics: Networks, Operating Systems, Patch
  Management, Security Best Practices, Security Controls, Security Incidents,
  Security Tools, and Servers.

name: MIG Welding
description:
The MIG Welding test measures knowledge of general welding information,
  like welding techniques and joint types, MIG welding variables, transfer
  mechanisms, and troubleshooting, and interpreting welding symbols.

name: Motivation Questionnaire (MQ.M5)
description:
By understanding what motivates their staff, managers can unlock each
  individual’s full potential and direct their energies more constructively.
  This questionnaire measures 18 dimensions of an individual’s motivation, and
  provides a comprehensive understanding of those situations which increase and
  reduce their motivation.

name:
Motivation Questionnaire (MQ.M5)
  Candidate Motivation Report
description:
The Candidate Motivation Report is designed as a feedback report for the
  individual. It provides an in-depth and easy-to-understand evaluation of an
  individual's motivators and demotivators at work.

name:
Motivation Questionnaire (MQ.M5) Employee
  Motivation Report
description:
The MQ Employee Motivation Report is ideal for use by line managers and
  those concerned with employee’s performance and well being, the Employee
  Motivation Report provides an in-depth and easy-to-understand evaluation of
  what motivates and de-motivates someone. It also offers a comprehensive list
  of tips and suggestions for managing the employee’s strongest motivators and
  demotivators.

name:
Motivation Questionnaire (MQ.M5) Premium
  Motivation Pack
description:
The MQ Report pack consists of the Profile Chart, Employee Motivation
  Report and the Candidate Report and is a cost effective way of ensuring that
  your organisation takes a comprehensive approach to understanding what
  motivates its employees.

name: Motivation Questionnaire (MQ.M5) Profile
description:
The profile chart is the Sten score graphical output of the MQ. It
  clearly shows the individual’s motivational drivers compared to the selected
  norm group as well as highlighting any unique scores.

name: Multiple Virtual Storage (MVS)
description:
The Multiple Virtual Storage (MVS) test measures knowledge of programming
  in an IBM MVS environment. Designed for experienced programmers and
  administrators, this test covers the following topics: Data Management, Data
  Structures, Job Control Language, System Abends, Transaction Systems and
  Concepts, TSO, and VSAM.

name: Natural
description:
The Natural test measures knowledge of programming in Natural. Designed
  for experienced programmers, this test covers the following topics: Break
  Processing, Calls, Conditional Processing, Copycode, Database Access, Define
  Data, Inputting Data, Mathematical Operations, Natural Fundamentals,
  Non-database File Access, Outputting Data, String Operations, and
  Subroutines.

name: Network Authentication
description:
The Network Authentication test measures knowledge of authentication
  protocols, policies, and configuration and audit techniques. Designed for
  experienced network administrators, this test covers the following topics:
  Authentication Methods,  Encryption,
  Network Design and Architecture, Protocols, Risk Mitigation, User Accounts,
  Virtual Private Networking (VPN), and Web Server Authentication.

name: Network Monitoring
description:
The Network Monitoring test measures knowledge of the core aspects of
  monitoring and troubleshooting networks. Designed for experienced network
  administrators, this test covers the following topics: Architecture and
  Terminology, Other Monitoring and Troubleshooting Tools, RMON Background,
  Components, and Commands, SNMP Background, Components, and Commands, System
  Design and Implementation, and Using SNMP and RMON Tools.

name: Network Technical Support
description:
The Network Technical Support test measures knowledge of setup,
  configuration, and maintenance of a local area network. Designed for
  technicians with hands on-experience, this test covers the following topics:
  Hardware/Media, LAN Support, Network Design, Services, System Administration,
  System Security, WAN Support, WLAN Support, and Workstation Support.

name: Networking Concepts
description:
The Networking Concepts test measures knowledge of the core aspects of
  local area networks, whether Windows NT or Novell. Designed for experienced
  network administrators, this test covers the following topics:
  Administration, Design, LAN Topologies, Network Devices, Network OS, Network
  Standards, Protocol Stacks, Routing Protocols, Troubleshooting, and WAN
  Topologies.

name: Novell NetWare 5.0 Administration
description:
The Novell NetWare 5.0 Administration test measures knowledge of
  administering a network running Novell NetWare 5.0 network operating system.
  Designed for experienced network administrators, this test covers the
  following topics: Administration, Backup Services, Directory Services,
  Installation and Configuration, Login, Partitions and Replicas, Printing,
  Security, Server Management, Time Synchronization, Utilities, and ZEN Works.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Candidate Plus Report
description:
A brief narrative OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report
  designed to be given to the individual who completed the OPQ.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Candidate Report
description:
A brief narrative OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report
  structure around 3 OPQ profile sections which can be given to candidates to
  keep after a feedback session

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Development and Action Planner Report (UCF)
description:
This OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report provides clear
  and relevant information about an individual's strengths and areas for
  development, for use in staff development and on-boarding situations.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Emotional Intelligence Report
description:
This OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report explores how a
  person manages feelings and relationships with other people.  It is intended for use in a development
  setting.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Leadership Report
description:
This OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report provides a
  detailed analysis of an individual's leadership potential.  It is based on SHL's leading edge
  Leadership Model, providing a competency based approach to leadership.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Manager Plus Report
description:
This concise OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report is
  designed for use with and by managers. 
  It uses clear succinct bullets and tables for ease of
  interpretation.  It provide simple
  comments on each of the personality traits.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Maximise Your Learning Report
description:
This OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report is designed to
  help people get the most from their development.  It summarises the preferred approach to
  learning across four dimensions.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Premium Plus Report
description:
This OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report contains a
  selection of reports including OPQ profile, user report, manager plus report,
  candidate plus report, universal competency report, team impact selection
  report.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Profile
description:
This is a graphical profile charts presenting results across the 32 OPQ
  scales. It is designed to be interpreted by OPQ trained users only.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Sales Report
description:
This OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report provides a
  graphical and narrative summary of an individual's natural style that is
  critical to sales success. It can also, optionally, use input from the SHL
  Motivation Questionnaire (MQ) to add information about the sales motivators
  and drives of an individual.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Team Impact Report Selection Report
description:
These reports are based on the SHL Team Impact Model, which is focused on
  actual team processes.  They are
  supported by clear and easy to understand graphics which do not require OPQ
  training.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Team Types and Leadership Styles Profile
description:
This OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report is based on
  Belbin's team types and Bass's leadership and reporting styles.  Belbins team types: individual preferred
  role when working in ateam.  Bass's
  leadership and reporting styles: individuals preferred leadership styles and
  likely style of behaviour as a direct report.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Team Types and Leadership Styles Report
description:
This OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report is based on
  Belbin's team types and Bass's leadership and reporting styles.  Belbins team types: individual preferred
  role when working in ateam.  Bass's
  leadership and reporting styles: individuals preferred leadership styles and
  likely style of behaviour as a direct report.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) Universal Competency Report (UCF)
description:
This OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report is based on the
  Universal Competency framework. It graphically outlines how an individual's
  typical way of behaving is likely to impact on competencies. It provides a
  graphical scale for each competency and summarizes aspects of personality
  which contribute (positively or negatively) to each competency.

name:
Occupational Personality Questionnaire
  (OPQ - OPQ32) User Report
description:
This OPQ (Occupational Personality Questionnaire) report includes a
  Profile Chart and narrative text, focusing on an individuals likely way of
  behaving at work.  It can be used as an
  interpretation aid when giving feedback, writing reports, or interpreting OPQ
  information.

name: Office Management
description:
This test measures ability to manage a business office. It is designed
  for experienced professionals and covers the following topics: Interpersonal
  Communication, Administration, Organization, Clerical, Technology, Human
  Resources, Finance, Training and Development, and Facility Management.

name: Office Procedures
description:
This test measures knowledge of typical administrative and support
  activities performed in offices today. It is designed for all support and
  administrative professionals and covers the following topics: Computer,
  Correspondence, Customer Service, Filing, Organization, Proofreading,
  Scheduling, and Telephone Techniques.

name: OO Concepts
description:
The OO Concepts test measures knowledge of the most important aspects of
  object-oriented (OO) programming. Designed for both programmers and
  administrators, this test covers the following topics:
  Architectures/Programming, Classes and Objects, Design, GUIs and Class
  Libraries, Languages, Persistence and Concurrency, and Process and Practices.

name: OO Design Patterns
description:
The OO Design Patterns test measures knowledge of working with and
  employing design patterns. Designed for experienced object-oriented
  programmers, this test covers the following topics: Distributed and
  Persistence Patterns, Fundamental Architectural Patterns, GoF Patterns, and
  Language Specific Patterns.

name: OS/400
description:
The OS/400 test measures knowledge of working within OS/400, IBM's AS/400
  operating system. Designed for experienced AS/400 users, this test covers the
  following topics: Communications, Data, Devices, IPL/MI, Jobs, Messages,
  Performance/Maintenance, Printing, and Security.

name: Payroll Fundamentals
description:
This test measures knowledge of processing receivables, invoices, and
  payroll.  Designed for payroll clerks
  and others who handle payroll processing, this test covers the following
  topics: Bookkeeping, Fair Labor Standards Act, Payroll Calculation, Tax
  Forms, and Worker Status. A calculator is needed when taking this exam.

name: Perl
description:
The Perl test measures knowledge of programming in an earlier version
  (5.0) of Practical Extraction Report Language (PERL). Designed for
  experienced programmers, this test covers the following topics: Basics,
  Functions, Ions, Literals, Modules, Statements, and Subroutines.

name: Perl 5.8
description:
The Perl 5.8 test measures knowledge of programming in Practical
  Extraction Reporting Language (PERL) 5.8. Designed for experienced
  programmers, this test covers the following topics: Data Manipulation, Data
  Structures, Input/Output, Modules, Object-Oriented Programming, Programming
  Methodologies, Statements, Subroutines, and Variables.

name: Programmer/Analyst Aptitude
description:
The Programmer/Analyst Aptitude test measures knowledge of programming
  and business. Designed for experienced programmers and business analysts,
  this test covers the following topics: Abstract Thinking, Analytical
  Reasoning, Attention to Detail, Mathematical Problem-Solving, and Process
  Mapping.

name: Proofreading
description:
This assessment measures ability to read for errors involving spelling,
  punctuation, grammar and word choice. 
  It also measures ability to correct those errors.

name: Python 2.4
description:
The Python 2.4 test measures knowledge of programming using Python 2.4.
  Designed for experienced programmers, this test covers the following topics:
  Built-in Features, Classes, Functions, Language, Logical and Physical
  Organization, and Standard Library.

name: RDBMS Concepts
description:
The RDBMS Concepts test measures knowledge of the most important aspects
  of the Relational Database Management System. Designed for programmers and
  administrators, this test covers the following topics: Data Modeling, Design,
  Normalization, Performance, Procedures and Transactions, and SQL.

name: Reviewing Forms (R1)
description:
This assessment measures ability to detect errors in forms by comparing
  text in a form to text in a paragraph format. Based on information in the
  paragraph, the test taker must select the answer that indicates the incorrect
  information.    This test is available
  in US English and UK English.

name: RPG IV
description:
The RPG IV test measures knowledge of programming using Report Programmer
  Generator (RPG) IV. Designed for experienced programmers, this test covers
  the following topics: Arrays/Tables, Built-in Functions, Data Types/Formats,
  Defining Data/Prototypes, Exception/Error Handling, Expressions, Form
  Specifications, Indicators, Operation Codes, Programming Cycle,
  Subprocedures, Subroutines, and Symbolic Names/Reserved Words.

name: SAP Basis Administration
description:
The SAP Basis Administration test measures knowledge of SAP Basis
  Administration. Designed for experienced users, this test covers the
  following topics: Backup and Recovery, Batch Processing, CCMS, Change
  Management, Client, Security, Software Components, and WEBAS Installation.

name: SAS 8.2 (Base)
description:
The SAS 8.2 (Base) test measures knowledge of the base package of SAS
  8.2. Designed for experienced programmers, this test covers the following
  topics: Data Aggregation and Reshaping, Data Step Programming Logic, Data
  Types and Transformation, External Files I/O, Grouped Data and BY-Processing
  Logic, Reports, SAS Data Set Combination and Modification, SAS Program
  Organization, Single SAS Data Set Input, and Table Look-Up.

name: Scenarios Executive
description:
Executive Scenarios is a unique test of Managerial Judgement - an
  individual’s ability to decide on effective ways of handling real life
  situations. It is specifically designed to assess judgement in Executives; a
  group who typically has significant work experience to draw on. It consists
  of hypothetical scenarios, each followed by several possible responses.
  Candidates rate the effectiveness of each response, using a 6-point scale,
  from highly undesirable to highly desirable. It can be used for recruitment,
  selection, training and development, performance management and coaching in a
  variety of functions and industries.

name: Scenarios Executive Profile
description:
Displays all scores on these scales in terms of four types of norm
  system; T-scores, Percentiles, Stens and Grades.

name: Scenarios Graduate
description:
Graduate Scenarios is a unique test of Managerial Judgement - an
  individual’s ability to decide on effective ways of handling real life
  situations. It is specifically designed to assess judgement in graduates; a
  group who typically has limited work experience to draw on. It consists of
  hypothetical scenarios, each followed by several possible responses.
  Candidates rate the effectiveness of each response, using a 6-point scale,
  from highly undesirable to highly desirable. It can be used for recruitment, selection,
  training and development, performance management and coaching in a variety of
  functions and industries.

name: Scenarios Graduate Profile
description:
Displays all scores on these scales in terms of four types of norm
  system; T-scores, Percentiles, Stens and Grades.

name: Scenarios Management 2012
description:
Managerial Scenarios is a unique test of Managerial Judgement - an
  individual’s ability to decide on effective ways of handling real life
  situations. It consists of hypothetical scenarios, each followed by several
  possible responses. Candidates rate the effectiveness of each response, using
  a 6-point scale, from highly undesirable to highly desirable. It can be used
  for recruitment, selection, training and development, performance management
  and coaching in a variety of functions and industries.

name: Scenarios Management 2012 Profile
description:
Displays all scores on these scales in terms of four types of norm
  system; T-scores, Percentiles, Stens and Grades.

name: SEI Cap Maturity Model Integr (CMMI)
description:
The SEI Capability Maturity Model Integration (CMMI) test measures
  knowledge of SEI CMMI. Designed for experienced users, this test covers the
  following topics: Implementation, Process Areas for Level 2: Managed, Process
  Areas for Level 3: Defined, Process Areas for Level 4 and Level 5, Process
  Components and Overviewing, Relationships Among Process Areas, and Structure.

name: Server Administration
description:
The Server Administration test measures knowledge of administering a
  server using any platform. Designed for experienced administrators, this test
  covers the following topics: Disaster Recovery, Hardware, Management Process,
  Network Addressing and Routing, Optimization, Security, Storage, and Users
  and Resources.

name: SOAP
description:
The SOAP test measures knowledge of programming and working with Web
  services using SOAP. Designed for experienced users, this test covers the
  following topics: Attachments, Encodings, Error Handling, Messaging, SOAP
  Concepts, SOAP Framework, SOAP Special Features, Web Services, and XML. This
  test is based on SOAP 1.1 W3C.

name: Software Configuration Management
description:
The Software Configuration Management test measures knowledge of how to
  practice the principles of configuration management during large software
  projects and when performing enhancements and maintenance on a continuous
  basis. Designed for experienced configuration managers, this test covers the
  following topics: CM Basics, CM Data, CM Planning, Configuration Audits,
  Configuration Control, Configuration Identification, Configuration Status
  Accounting, Defect Tracking, and General Knowledge.

name: Software Quality Assurance
description:
The Software Quality Assurance test measures knowledge of maintaining
  software quality throughout the development lifecycle. Designed for software
  quality controllers and software developers, this test covers the following
  topics: Analysis, Configuration Management, Managing SQA Projects, Philosophy
  Behind SQA, Processes, Standards, and Metrics, Testing, and Verification and
  Validation.

name: Software Testing
description:
The Software Testing test measures knowledge of testing software.
  Designed for experienced software testers, this test covers the following
  topics: Dynamic Techniques, Fundamentals, Levels, Management, Monitoring,
  Static Techniques, Tools, and Types.

name: Split Screen Typing Test - Form 1
description: This test measures speed and accuracy in typing text presented on the computer screen. In this assessment, the text original is displayed directly above the area in which the test taker must enter the response. The test taker will not need a printed original for the evaluation. This test calculates a score based on the total number of keystrokes, time taken, and number of errors made when typing six passages. This Split Screen Typing Test uses the following method to determine the Net Words Per Minute score: Net Words Per Minute = ((Gross Words Per Minute * Time Taken) - Total Errors) / Time Taken.

name: SQL (ANSI)
description:
The SQL (ANSI) test measures knowledge of database queries using ANSI
  SQL. Designed for experienced database programmers and administrators, this
  test covers the following topics: Relational Database and SQL Basics, SQL
  Data Modification, SQL Queries, and Using SQL DML.

name: SQL (ANSI) Fundamentals
description:
The SQL (ANSI) Fundamentals test measures knowledge of database queries
  using ANSI SQL. Designed for entry-level database programmers and
  administrators, this test covers the following topics: Grouping and Totaling,
  Relational Database and SQL Basics, SQL Data Definition (DDL), SQL Data
  Modification, SQL Queries, and Using SQL DML.

name: Storage Area Networks Concepts
description:
The Storage Area Networks (SAN) Concepts test measures knowledge of
  implementing, maintaining, and architecting storage area networks. Designed
  for experienced system administrators, storage administrators, and system
  architects/engineers who are responsible for storage of information data,
  this test covers the following topics: Clusters, Data Transports, Disaster
  Recovery, Management, Network Considerations, Replication, Security, and
  Troubleshooting.

name: TCP/IP Administration
description:
The TCP/IP Administration test measures knowledge of administering a
  network using the Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol (TCP/IP).
  Designed for experienced Network Administrators, this test covers the
  following topics: DHCP/BOOTP, DNS, General Knowledge, IP Addressing, IP
  Security, IPv6, Routing, Subnetting, TCP/IP Standards, TCP/IP Suite,
  Troubleshooting, and WINS.

name: Technical Helpdesk (Microsoft)
description:
The Technical Help Desk (Microsoft) test measures knowledge of Technical
  Help Desk (Microsoft). Designed for experienced users, this test covers the
  following topics: Hardware, Help Desk Operations, Internet Explorer,
  Microsoft Office, Networking, Operating Systems, PC Peripherals, Software,
  and System Security.

name: Time Management
description:
This is an adaptive test that measures the candidate's knowledge of how
  to use time wisely in the workplace. It is designed for the average business
  worker and covers the following topics: Action Plans, Controlling, Decision
  Making, Organizing, Planning, Scheduling, Time Usage, and Time Wasters.

name: Typing Test 1
description:
This assessment is timed and designed to measure speed and accuracy in
  typing. This assessment allows the 5 minutes to type passages into a
  simulated word processing document. Test takers will need a printed text
  original for this type of evaluation. The reading level of this assessment
  ranges from the 10th to 12th grades. This typing test uses the following
  formula to determine the Net Words Per Minute score: Net Words Per Minute =
  Gross Words Per Minute - Total Errors. 
  Localized for use in the UK.

name: Typing Test 2
description:
This assessment is timed and designed to measure speed and accuracy in
  typing. This assessment allows the 5 minutes to type passages into a
  simulated word processing document. Test takers will need a printed text
  original for this type of evaluation. The reading level of this assessment
  ranges from the 10th to 12th grades. This typing test uses the following
  formula to determine the Net Words Per Minute score: Net Words Per Minute =
  Gross Words Per Minute - Total Errors.  
  Localized for use in the UK.

name: Typing Test 3
description:
This assessment is timed and designed to measure speed and accuracy in
  typing. This assessment allows the 5 minutes to type passages into a
  simulated word processing document. Test takers will need a printed text
  original for this type of evaluation. The reading level of this assessment
  ranges from the 10th to 12th grades. This typing test uses the following
  formula to determine the Net Words Per Minute score: Net Words Per Minute =
  Gross Words Per Minute - Total Errors.  
  Localized for use in the UK.

name: Unified Modeling Language (UML)
description:
The Unified Modeling Language (UML) test measures knowledge of Unified
  Modeling Language (UML). Designed for experienced IT professionals, this test
  covers the following topics: Architectural Modeling, Behavioral Modeling,
  Class Diagrams, Structural Modeling, and Use Cases.

name: Unix Administration (Solaris 10)
description:
The UNIX Administration (Solaris 10) test measures knowledge of Sun's
  Solaris implementation of UNIX. Designed for experienced administrators, this
  test covers the following topics: Automation, Debug Tools and Methods, Files
  and Filesystems, Network, Network Services, Packages and Patches, Performance
  and Capacity, Process Control and Life Cycle, Security, and Virtualization.

name: Unix Korn Shell Scripting
description:
The UNIX Korn Shell Scripting test measures knowledge of Korn Shell
  Scripting. Designed for scripting professionals, this test covers the
  following topics: Built-in Script Commands, Command-Line Editing, Flow
  Control, Functions, Integer Variables and Arithmetic, Korn Shell Basics,
  Script Creation and Execution, Script Debugging, Script I/O, Script
  Variables, and Shell Initialization.

name: Unix Programming (General)
description:
The UNIX Programming (General) test measures knowledge of programming in

  the following topics: Design, Compile, and Profile Code, Files and
  Directories, I/O, Interprocess Communication, Language Concepts, Process
  Control, and Process Relationships.

name: VBScript 5.5
description:
The VBScript 5.5 test measures knowledge of programming using VBScript
  5.5. Designed for experienced programmers, this test covers the following
  topics: Objects, Operators, Principles of VBScript Functions/Statements,

name: Verbal Reasoning (R1)
description:
This assessment measures ability to combine separate pieces of
  information and determine true statements based on that information. The test
  taker is required to review a list of facts and select the correct conclusion
  from a list of four statements.    The
  test is available in US English, UK English and Australasian English.

  work of others, are required. The test is relevant to entry-level,
  administrative and clerical roles, as well as apprenticeship schemes.

name: Visual Basic 6.0 Fundamentals
description:
The Visual Basic 6.0 Fundamentals test measures knowledge of programming
  in Visual Basic 6.0. Designed for entry-level programmers, this test covers
  the following topics: Application Design, Common Windows Objects, IDE Tools,
  Object-Oriented Coding, Primary Language Features, User-Defined Procedures,
  and User Interface Objects.

name: Visual Basic.NET
description:
The Visual Basic.NET test measures knowledge of programming with Visual
  Basic using the .NET environment. Designed for experienced programmers, this
  test covers the following topics: Classes/Inheritance, Consuming Resources,
  Data Access, Debugging, Distributed Applications, Language Fundamentals, Web
  Forms, and Windows Forms.

name: Visual Basic.NET Fundamentals
description:
The Visual Basic.NET Fundamentals test measures knowledge of programming
  with Visual Basic using the .NET framework. Designed for entry-level
  programmers, this test covers the following topics: .NET Framework, Classes
  and Inheritances, Control Flow, Data Access, Troubleshooting, VB.NET
  Language, Variables and Types, Web Forms, and Windows Forms.

name: Visual C++
description:
The Visual C++ test measures knowledge of programming using Visual C++.
  Designed for experienced programmers, this test covers the following topics:
  ActiveX Controls, General C++ Language, General Visual C++, General Windows
  Programming, MFC, OLE/COM, and Visual C++ IDE.

name: Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP)
description:
The Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) test measures knowledge of the
  technology used to transmit voice conversations over a data network using the
  Internet Protocol, better known as Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP).
  Designed for experienced professionals, this test covers the following
  topics: Application, Hardware, IP Telephony Basics, Protocols, Quality of
  Service, and Standards.

name: VSAM
description:
The VSAM test measures knowledge of the different architectures and
  technologies used in Wide Area Networks. Designed for experienced network
  administrators, this test covers the following topics: Backup and
  Reorganization, Design, General, IDCAMS, Listcat, Performance, Security, and
  Structure.

name: WAN Technologies
description:
The WAN Technologies test measures knowledge of the different
  architectures and technologies used in Wide Area Networks (WAN). Designed for
  experienced network administrators, this test covers the following topics:
  Basic Knowledge, Choosing WAN Service, RAS, Routing, WAN Connections, WAN
  Methodology, WAN Support, and WAN Types.

name: Web Design for Accessibility
description:
The Web Design for Accessibility test measures knowledge of designing and
  maintaining a website that is easily accessible for those with disabilities.
  Designed for Web designers and administrators, this test covers the following
  topics: Assistive Technology, Basic Principles, CSS Techniques, Emerging
  Technologies, General Knowledge, HTML Techniques, Programming Languages, and
  WAI Guidelines.

name: Web Development Concepts
description:
This adaptive test measures the candidate's knowledge of the core areas
  common to all Web development, whether it be client-side or server-side. It
  is designed for experienced Web developers and includes the following topics:
  programming concepts, object oriented development concepts, internet
  architecture and protocols, HTML, and XML.

name: Web Server Administration
description:
The Web Server Administration test measures knowledge of the key issues
  and concepts in administering a Web server. Designed for Web administrators,
  this test covers the following topics: Connectivity Issues, Data Integrity,
  General Knowledge, Monitoring, Planning, Protocols, Resource Access, Server
  Optimization, Server Security, and Troubleshooting.

name: Web Services Application Engineering
description:
The Web Services Application Engineering test measures knowledge of Web
  Services implementation. Designed for experienced Web architects, software
  engineers, and application developers, this test covers the following topics:
  Architecture and Design, Completing Web Services, Security, SOAP, SOAP-RPC,
  UDDI, Web Services Overview, WSDL, and XML Implementation.

name: Web Services Concepts
description:
The Web Services Concepts test measures knowledge of Web Services.
  Designed for experienced Web architects, software engineers, and application
  developers, this test covers the following topics: Application Design,
  Application Implementation, Architecture, Major Frameworks, Protocol/API
  Groups, Roles, Service Description and Discovery Layers, and Transport and
  XML Messaging Layers.

name: WebSphere MQ
description:
The WebSphere MQ test measures knowledge of WebSphere MQ. Designed for
  application engineers, architects, and programmers, this test covers the
  following topics: Administration, Application Programming, Commands,
  Intercommunication and Queue Manager Clusters, MQSeries Concepts, and
  Planning and Installation.

name: Windows Application Program Interface
description:
The Windows Application Program Interface test measures knowledge of
  programming using the Windows Application Program Interface (API). Designed
  for experienced programmers, this test covers the following topics: Graphics,
  System, and User Interface.

name: WinRunner 6.0
description:
The WinRunner 6.0 test measures knowledge of the Mercury Interactive
  WinRunner software. Designed for experienced technical managers and
  engineers, this test covers the following topics: Checkpoints, GUI Map,
  Modular Test Tree, Record/Run, and Synchronization.

name: Word Processing Fundamentals
description:
This test demonstrates that the candidate has the skills necessary to
  proficiently use the most popular business applications at a user level. The
  test is focused on the topics used daily by most professionals. The Word
  Processing Fundamentals test covers the following topics: Advanced Features,
  Basic Settings, Document Creation, Document Exchange, Formatting, Page
  Layout, Revision, and Shortcuts.

name: Apache Pig (New)

name: Apache Spark (New)

name: ASP .NET with C# (New)

This test includes the following topics: .NET Framework, Client-Side Programming, Data Access, Enhanced Runtime Features, Portals, Services and Mobile, Security, Troubleshooting and Optimization, Web Applications, and Web Forms.

name: Management Scenarios
description:
Managerial Scenarios is a unique test of Managerial Judgement - an individual’s ability to decide on effective ways of handling real life situations. It consists of hypothetical scenarios, each followed by several possible responses. Candidates rate the effectiveness of each response, using a 6-point scale, from highly undesirable to highly desirable. It can be used for recruitment, selection, training and development, performance management and coaching in a variety of functions and industries.

Reports available:

Management Scenarios Narrative Report:  A participant focused report that contains;  
 - Management Scenarios profile
 - Detailed narrative feedback of each of the 4 scales;  Managerial Judgement, Managing Objectives, People Management and Reputation Management.
 - Table of scores for each of the 6 style scales;  Big picture, Delegative, One-to-one, Team, Personal recognition and Company Protocol.
 - Detailed narrative feedback report (with development tips) for each of the 6 style scales.

Management Scenarios Candidate Report:  A report to share with the candidate that contains: 
 - Detailed narrative feedback of each of the 4 scales;  Managerial Judgement, Managing Objectives, People Management and Reputation Management.
 - Table of scores for each of the 6 style scales;  Big picture, Delegative, One-to-one, Team, Personal recognition and Company Protocol.
 - Detailed narrative feedback report (with development tips) for each of the 6 style scales.

Management Scenarios Profile:  Displays all scores on these scales in terms of four types of norm system; T-scores, Percentiles, Stens and Grades.

name: Customer Service Phone Solution

It clearly shows the individual’s motivational drivers compared to the selected norm group as well as highlighting any unique scores.Please note this report is currently being updated on Talent Central as part of a reporting refresh project, and there may be visual differences when comparing Talent Central reports to ones generated on other assessment platforms. Differences are regarding the layout and display of the reports only and do not affect the underlying scoring. When viewing sample reports, please ensure you select the appropriate language and platform example.Where a sample report has no platform indicated, it is available on both the Talent Central and SODA platforms.

name: Multitasking Ability
description: The Multitasking Ability assessment is a measure of one’s ability to adeptly work on more than one task simultaneously, while maintaining efficiency and effectiveness when interrupted or switching between tasks.  This test is a face-valid, split-screen simulation that is designed to assess multitasking ability. It captures the dynamic nature of the working environment by presenting the candidate with multiple types of items in a timed format. Candidates will be required to complete problem-solving items that are presented on one side of the screen, while at the same time attending to emails that are presented within an email inbox on the other side of the screen.

name: Networking and Implementation (New)
